summary.intent: Add a second Z.AI provider (`zai-code`) targeting the Coding plan endpoint to fix 'insufficient balance' errors for Z.AI Coding plan users (GitHub issue #414).
summary.outcome: Successfully added `zai-code` provider across all 5 required files, all tests pass, formatting is clean, and changes were committed and pushed with a PR created.

repo: Adding a new OpenAI-compatible provider requires changes in 5 places: crates/providers/src/lib.rs, crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs, crates/config/src/provider_env.rs, crates/config/src/template.rs, and docs/src/providers.md
Provider test arrays in provider-setup/src/lib.rs must be updated in 4 places when adding a new KnownProvider
Z.AI models are referenced via a shared ZAI_MODELS constant, so the new zai-code provider reuses the same model list
The project uses a flat provider pattern (no smart auto-detection) — separate provider entries for plan variants is the established convention per Cstewart-HC

code.finding: OpenAiCompatDef for zai-code inserted after existing zai entry; uses Z_CODE_API_KEY and https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4
Test zai_code_registers_with_api_key added after zai_registers_with_api_key following the same pattern
KnownProvider for zai-code with display_name 'Z.AI (Coding Plan)' added after zai entry
Env key mapping ('zai-code', 'Z_CODE_API_KEY') added alongside existing ('zai', 'Z_API_KEY')
zai-code added to commented provider list for discoverability

workflow: cargo test with a hyphenated filter (e.g., 'zai-code') won't match test functions with underscores — use underscore filter or full function name instead
The project uses 'just lint' for clippy and 'cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all -- --check' for formatting verification
Background task completion notifications require reading the output file to retrieve results

friction: Background test task used a hyphenated filter ('zai') that matched zero test names because Rust test functions use underscores — required re-running tests with corrected filter names
Had to poll the background task multiple times waiting for workspace compilation to complete before seeing test results
open_items: No smart auto-detection of Z.AI plan type — users must manually choose between 'zai' and 'zai-code' providers; this was intentionally deferred per Cstewart-HC's recommendation
Documentation update limited to providers.md table row — may need more detailed guidance on when Coding plan users should use zai-code vs zai
